seo.title: Luma Ray 2 Specs, Pricing and Legacy Workflows | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use Luma Ray 2 in MaxVideoAI as a previous-generation Luma route for cinematic shots, image-to-video, source-video modify, and delivery reframes. Start new Luma work on Ray 3.2 when Modify keyframes or Reframe matter.

overview: Luma Ray 2 is a previous-generation Luma route in MaxVideoAI. Keep it for legacy prompts, older comparisons, and Ray 2-compatible generation, while new Luma video work should start on Ray 3.2 for Modify keyframes and Reframe.

hero.title: Luma Ray 2 - cinematic generation, modify, and reframe on one page
hero.intro: Use Ray 2 when you need to revisit older Luma prompts or keep Ray 2-compatible text-to-video, image-to-video, source-video Modify, and Reframe runs. For new Luma production, Ray 3.2 is the current route.
hero.badge: Legacy Luma route - T2V + I2V + Modify + Reframe

items.title: Source-video look changes without new blocking

technicalOverview.label: Generate workflows
technicalOverview.body: Text-to-Video and Image-to-Video with 5 s or 9 s clips

technicalOverview.label: Edit workflows
technicalOverview.body: Modify and Reframe use an uploaded source video instead of fixed 5 s or 9 s presets

technicalOverview.label: Aspect ratios
technicalOverview.body: 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, 21:9, and 9:21 on generate; Reframe also adds 1:1

technicalOverview.label: Image controls
technicalOverview.body: Image-to-Video supports one start image plus an optional end image; Modify and Reframe can also take an optional reference still

technicalOverview.label: Modify options
technicalOverview.body: Modify presets exposed in MaxVideoAI: adhere_1 to adhere_3, flex_1 to flex_3, and reimagine_1 to reimagine_3

technicalOverview.label: Reframe options
technicalOverview.body: Target aspect ratio plus optional grid anchors and crop-window coordinates

technicalOverview.label: Pricing
technicalOverview.body: Displayed pricing starts around $0.50 for 5 s at 540p on generate, about $0.12/s for Modify, and about $0.20/s for Reframe

promptStructure.title: How to prompt Luma Ray 2 by workflow
promptStructure.quote: Mode: Modify. Goal: keep the camera path and timing from the source clip. Change: metallic silk wardrobe, cooler highlights, cleaner showroom background. Strength: flex_2.
promptStructure.description: Treat Ray 2 as four related workflows, not one generic prompt box. The right prompt depends on whether you are generating a new shot, animating a still, modifying an existing clip, or reframing one for a new format.
steps: For Text-to-Video, write one clear subject, one camera move, one lighting direction, then choose duration, resolution, and format.
For Image-to-Video, let the uploaded still anchor the first frame and use the prompt to define motion and scene evolution.
Add an end image only when the final composition matters more than free-form motion.
For Modify, tell Ray 2 what must stay from the source clip and what should change, then pick the right adherence or reimagine preset.
For Reframe, describe the priority subject and intended crop outcome instead of rewriting the whole scene.
Use short, shot-level language. Ray 2 performs best when the instruction is cinematic but controlled.

tips.title: Tips and limits
items: Use Ray 2 when quality matters more than raw iteration speed.
Start in Text-to-Video or Image-to-Video, then move into Modify only when the blocking is already working.
Use adhere presets when continuity matters; move toward flex or reimagine only when you want stronger visual change.
Use Reframe for delivery variants, especially 9:16 and 1:1 crops derived from an approved landscape master.
Keep generate runs for net-new shots and edit runs for preserving strong footage while changing styling or format.

prompts.title: Text-to-Video luxury lobby shot
prompts.prompt: Elegant boutique hotel lobby at dusk, warm practical lighting reflecting on polished marble, slow dolly forward, restrained cinematic pacing, 9s, 16:9 at 1080p.

prompts.title: Image-to-Video editorial still
prompts.prompt: Animate the uploaded editorial still into a subtle forward move with soft fabric motion and stable facial identity, keep the studio lighting premium, 5s.

prompts.title: Modify with style shift
prompts.prompt: Keep the source clip timing and camera path, refresh the wardrobe into metallic silk, cool the highlights, simplify the set dressing, flex_2.
prompts.notes: Upload the source video first, then choose the modify preset that matches the strength of the change.

prompts.title: Vertical reframe for social
prompts.prompt: Reframe the uploaded landscape fashion clip into a clean 9:16 crop, keep the face and shoulders prioritized, maintain motion continuity across the full shot.

faqs.q: What workflows does Luma Ray 2 expose in MaxVideoAI?
faqs.a: Ray 2 keeps the active Luma workflow family inside one model page: text-to-video, image-to-video, modify, and reframe.

faqs.q: Which options are exposed in Modify?
faqs.a: Modify uses a source video plus an optional prompt and optional reference still. MaxVideoAI exposes strength presets from adhere_1 through reimagine_3.

faqs.q: Which options are exposed in Reframe?
faqs.a: Reframe uses a source video, a target aspect ratio, an optional prompt, an optional reference still, plus optional grid and crop-window controls.

faqs.q: When should I choose Ray 2 over Ray 2 Flash?
faqs.a: Choose Ray 2 when you want the flagship Luma tier for higher-confidence finals, while Ray 2 Flash is better for faster drafts and cheaper iteration.

custom.heroDesc1: Luma Ray 2 is no longer just a narrow text-to-video page inside MaxVideoAI. It now follows the same workflow-first pattern used on the richer LTX and Veo pages: one public model, multiple active Luma workflows.
custom.heroDesc2: That matters in production. You can generate a shot from text, animate a still, modify a winning source clip, and reframe the approved master for social without changing the model family or relearning the controls.
custom.heroSupportLine: Best for premium cinematic work when you want generation and source-video edits inside one Luma surface.
custom.whyTitle: Why Luma Ray 2 is strategically useful
heroHighlights: One model, real workflow coverage||Generate new clips, animate stills, modify existing footage, and reframe approved shots from the same Luma page.
Modify is not hidden anymore||The adherence, flex, and reimagine presets are exposed directly so teams can steer how far the edit should move from the source clip.
Reframe is practical, not cosmetic||Square and vertical outputs matter when the approved master has to ship across paid social, mobile, and commerce placements.
Cleaner family story||Ray 2 stays the premium tier, Ray 2 Flash stays the fast tier, but both models now expose the real public workflow surface.
custom.specTitle: Real specs - Luma Ray 2 in MaxVideoAI
custom.specNote: These specs describe the active Luma routing exposed in MaxVideoAI today, not a reduced placeholder surface.

specSections.title: Generate workflows
specSections.intro: Text-to-Video and Image-to-Video are the creation modes: 5 or 9 seconds, 540p to 1080p, multiple cinematic aspect ratios, optional loop, and optional end-image guidance for image-driven runs.
items: Use Text-to-Video for net-new cinematic beats.
Use Image-to-Video when the first frame must stay on-brand.
Add an end image only when the landing composition matters.
Use loop for ambient or product motion tests.

specSections.title: Modify and Reframe
specSections.intro: The workflow expansion is not just marketing copy. Ray 2 now exposes source-video editing directly, which changes how useful the model is in production.
items: Modify preserves timing and camera path while changing look, styling, or scene interpretation.
Adhere presets stay close to the source clip, while flex and reimagine allow more creative drift.
Reframe keeps the motion but changes delivery format, with square and vertical outputs supported.
Optional crop and grid controls help keep the priority subject inside the new frame.

custom.whatTitle: How Luma Ray 2 works in MaxVideoAI
custom.whatIntro1: Choose Luma Ray 2, then stay in Generate Video for text or still-led creation, or switch to Modify / Reframe when you are working from an uploaded source clip.
custom.whatIntro2: This keeps the family simple at the model level while still exposing the active Luma workflows where they belong: inside the model page and workspace.
whatFlowSteps: Pick Luma Ray 2 as the engine.
Stay on Generate Video for text and image workflows, or switch to Modify / Reframe for source-video edits.
On generate runs, choose 5s or 9s, then pick resolution and aspect ratio.
Upload the relevant asset for the active workflow: start image, optional end image, or source video.
For Modify, pick the right adherence or reimagine preset.
Review the live price before generation, then iterate only on the workflow that matters.
strengths: High-value Luma workflow surface: generate, modify, and reframe in one place.
Useful when premium cinematic quality matters but the delivery plan also requires source-video edits.
Better family clarity than exposing separate public modify or reframe pseudo-models.
boundaries: 5s and 9s plus 540p to 1080p apply to the generate modes, not to source-video edit durations.
Modify and Reframe still depend on the quality and clarity of the uploaded source clip.
Ray 2 is the premium tier, so Flash remains the better option for fast early-stage iteration.
custom.finalPara1: Luma Ray 2 is the premium half of the new two-model Luma family, but it now behaves like a complete workflow page instead of a reduced placeholder. That is the right compromise: simple model lineup, broad Luma workflow coverage.

hero.title: Luma Ray 2
hero.subtitle: Previous-generation Luma route for supported Generate, Modify and Reframe workflows.
hero.paragraph: Use Luma Ray 2 when you need a legacy-compatible Luma render or an older Ray 2 workflow. Start new Luma Modify, guide/keyframe and Reframe work on Ray 3.2, then keep Ray 2 for historical prompts, fallback checks and older examples.

features.title: Legacy Luma route
features.body: Use Ray 2 for older prompts, historical examples and compatibility checks.

features.title: Text or image start
features.body: Generate from a prompt or animate one still with optional end-frame guidance.

features.title: Modify source clips
features.body: Keep the blocking and timing that already work while changing look or detail.

features.title: Reframe delivery cuts
features.body: Turn an approved source clip into vertical, square or wide delivery variants.

features.title: 5s or 9s generate
features.body: Choose short controlled generation runs up to 1080p.

features.title: Ray 3.2 for new edits
features.body: Use Ray 3.2 first when guide frames, keyframes or current Reframe behavior matter.

decisionCards.title: Ray 2 or Ray 3.2?
decisionCards.body: Use Ray 3.2 for current Luma Modify, keyframe guidance and Reframe work. Keep Ray 2 for legacy prompts, old examples and compatibility coverage.

decisionCards.title: Modifying existing footage?
decisionCards.body: Use Ray 2 Modify only when you need the older route. For new source-video edits, Ray 3.2 should be the first Luma option.

decisionCards.title: Need stronger generation?
decisionCards.body: Use Seedance, Kling or Veo when the job is flagship generation, sound-capable video or broader reference control rather than a Luma legacy workflow.

referenceWorkflows.title: Text-to-video
referenceWorkflows.body: Write a compact subject, camera, lighting and motion brief for a new cinematic shot.

referenceWorkflows.title: Image-to-video
referenceWorkflows.body: Use one start image to anchor composition, product shape or identity before adding motion.

referenceWorkflows.title: End frame
referenceWorkflows.body: Add a closing image only when the landing pose or final composition matters.

referenceWorkflows.title: Modify
referenceWorkflows.body: Upload a source video, name what must stay, name what changes, then choose an adherence strength.

referenceWorkflows.title: Reframe
referenceWorkflows.body: Prioritize the subject and delivery format instead of rewriting the whole scene.

pricingCopy.title: Legacy Luma Ray 2 pricing at a glance
pricingCopy.subtitle: Preset Ray 2 generate totals by duration and resolution - use Ray 3.2 for current Luma edit and Reframe work.
pricingCopy.footnote: Generate pricing uses preset duration/resolution tiers. Modify and Reframe are quoted live from source-clip settings.

demo.title: Demo prompt — Luma Ray 2
demo.prompt:
9s Luma Ray 2 render in 16:9, silent output.
A premium product sits on a golden-hour cafe table with clean reflections and a soft background.
Slow forward dolly, stable camera, one readable motion beat, cinematic depth of field.
Preserve product framing, avoid unreadable text, added logos or overly fast motion.
For Modify/Reframe, keep the approved source motion and specify only what changes.

summary.subject: Product hero in a golden-hour cafe
summary.action: Camera glides toward the product and reveals reflections
summary.camera: Slow dolly, stable framing, shallow depth of field
summary.style: Premium cinematic look, controlled highlights, no added branding
summary.output: Silent Ray 2 render, ready for delivery review
